mechanism_narrative: A negative 3-month trend in industrial production signals falling real activity and lower near-term earnings expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:as of 2008-04-15 INDPRO latest visible vintage observation was February 2008
failure_condition: Reject if negative 3-month INDPRO momentum is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered walk-forward observations.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment-rate break above 5.0% marks deterioration in household income and recession risk, causing investors to reduce equity exposure and producing negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above 5.0% after being at or below 5.0% during the prior 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:as of 2008-04-15 UNRATE vintage value for March 2008 was 5.1
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have mean 20-day S&P 500 returns greater than the unconditional mean or if the downside hit rate is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp federal funds easing while inflation remains elevated signals that policy is reacting to growth stress rather than creating a benign discount-rate tailwind, producing negative 60-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines > 100bp over 3 months while CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation exceeds 3%
evidence_refs: fact:as of 2008-04-15 FEDFUNDS latest visible value was 2.61
failure_condition: Reject if the joint trigger delivers positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases or if the interaction coefficient is not negative in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A housing-starts collapse combined with weak real GDP growth signals a broad demand contraction, credit tightening, and falling cyclical earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1200 and GDPC1 year-over-year growth is below 2%
evidence_refs: fact:as of 2008-04-15 HOUST latest visible value was 1065
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not underperform non-triggered episodes by at least 1 percentage point over 60 trading days in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in retail sales indicates resilient household spending and stronger revenue expectations, causing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:as of 2008-04-15 RSAFS latest visible value for March 2008 was 381416
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month RSAFS momentum has a walk-forward Rank IC below 0.03 for 20-day returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate crossing below 3.0% delivers a short-horizon liquidity and discount-rate relief effect, causing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses below 3.0% for the first time in 12 months
evidence_refs: fact:as of 2008-04-15 FEDFUNDS latest visible value was 2.61
failure_condition: Reject if 5-day S&P 500 returns after the crossing are negative in more than 55% of walk-forward trigger events.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll contraction together with a rising unemployment rate confirms that labor income is deteriorating, causing lower consumption expectations and negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change < 0 and UNRATE rises > 0.3pp over 6 months
evidence_refs: fact:as of 2008-04-15 PAYEMS latest visible value for March 2008 was 137846
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-UNRATE trigger has a non-negative average 60-day excess return versus unconditional S&P 500 returns.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nominal retail sales during high CPI inflation signals price-driven spending rather than real demand growth, compressing real consumption and margins and producing negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth > 0 while CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation > 4%
evidence_refs: fact:as of 2008-04-15 CPIAUCSL latest visible value was 212.571
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the RSAFS-CPIAUCSL interaction term is not negative.
